name: blood-pressure-chart
title: Blood Pressure Chart
description: Systolic and diastolic over time, drawn as one observation — a filled range between the pair, with optional MAP.

props.name: data
props.description: `{ time, systolic, diastolic, map?, position? }`. Null values are not bridged.

props.name: systolicTarget / diastolicTarget
props.description: The caller's targets, drawn as labelled lines.

props.name: showMap
props.description: Plots mean arterial pressure if the caller supplies it — the component computes nothing.

clinicalNotes: Systolic and diastolic are one observation, so they are drawn as one mark: a filled band between the pair, with each bound also plotted. Two unrelated lines hide the thing a clinician reads a blood-pressure chart for — the pulse pressure narrowing or widening between them. Position and cuff site are caller-supplied annotations shown per reading rather than assumptions, because a standing measurement and a seated one are not interchangeable: a chart that silently mixes them can invent a postural drop or conceal one.
notes: Composes chart. The band is a Recharts range Area fed a [diastolic, systolic] tuple.
